commands.review_summary:
The PR adds safe record/array projection helpers and applies them to Plugin SDK provider auth/env registration, unified text catalog projection, paired provider API-key catalogs, and focused tests for unreadable rows.

PR surface: Source +124, Tests +159. Total +283 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, though I did not execute the failing path in the read-only checkout. Current main uses direct auth mapping, Object.entries, and model property reads, while the PR adds throwing-getter fixtures for those failure modes.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The protected maintainer label, draft state, and compatibility/auth-provider semantics make this a maintainer landing decision rather than a safe automated repair job.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- splits the provider/catalog hardening slice out of the larger fuzz quarry branch at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87141
- hardens Plugin SDK provider entry registration so one unreadable auth option is skipped without dropping healthy sibling auth/env entries
- hardens provider catalog projection so one unreadable provider or model row is skipped without erasing healthy sibling model catalog rows
- adds a small shared safe-record helper instead of keeping duplicate proxy/getter guards in each provider path
- intentionally leaves the deferred install catalog, setup registry, UI/status, and unrelated fuzz surfaces out of this PR

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 2 files passed, 14 tests passed after final rebase
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/shared/safe-record.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- branch diff scrubbed for the reported plugin name and private local paths: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: passed on AWS Crabbox run `run_b0f53ab221e0`, lease `cbx_17f5cf0dceaa`, after fixing the safe-record typecheck miss from the earlier failed run `run_dc890b728a4f`
- final no-conflict rebase onto current `origin/main` completed after the AWS gate; the later main commits did not touch this provider catalog/auth surface, and the focused tests/format/diff/scrub sanity checks stayed green

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: provider SDK/provider catalog projection no longer lets one bad plugin-owned provider, model, or auth row poison healthy sibling provider catalog or auth rows.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw worktree branch `fuzz-critical-provider-catalog-pruned-20260530`, then force-updated PR branch `fuzz-critical-provider-catalog-20260530`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest for `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts` and `src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts`, formatter, diff check, private-name scrub, autoreview, and remote `pnpm check:changed` through AWS Crabbox.

Evidence after fix: tests cover unreadable provider catalog entries, unreadable provider model rows, unreadable provider auth option rows, and unreadable paired provider catalog rows while proving healthy `mockplugin` siblings survive.

Observed result after fix: healthy provider model rows still project into the unified text catalog when adjacent `fuzzplugin` rows throw, and healthy auth/env entries still register when an adjacent provider auth option is unreadable.

What was not tested: a live third-party plugin process was not started for this split; coverage is focused unit/changed-gate proof of the SDK and provider catalog projection paths.
target.author: vincentkoc
